compiled_state.title: As a DSO plugin developer or agent, I can list ready-to-work tickets via ticket ready
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add `ticket ready` subcommand that lists tickets whose `ready_to_work` computation returns true — i.e., all direct blockers are closed or tombstoned. Optional filters narrow output to an epic or story scope.

**Why**: Agents and humans need a fast way to see "what can I work on next?" without subprocess-parsing `ticket deps` across every ticket. The underlying `ready_to_work` field is already computed on-demand in `ticket_graph/_graph.py:97-102` — this story exposes it as a first-class CLI surface, consistent with the verb-sugar pattern established by Epic 6f7a-4ba6.

**Scope**:
- IN: `.claude/scripts/dso ticket ready [--epic=<id>] [--story=<id>] [--format=llm]` command.
- IN: Output fields per ticket: id, type, title, priority, status, assignee (if set).
- IN: Thin-shim verb pattern — bash script delegates to the sourceable ticket-lib API from Epic 78fc-3858.
- IN: Scope filters: `--epic` restricts to descendants of an epic; `--story` restricts to descendants of a story.
- IN: Documentation update in `ticket-cli-reference.md` alongside the existing subcommand docs.
- OUT: Persistence of `ready_to_work` in compiled state — remains on-demand.
- OUT: UI changes — CLI-only.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `.claude/scripts/dso ticket ready` prints a list of all open tickets with `ready_to_work=true`, ordered by priority then creation time.
  ← Satisfies: "Absorb ready computation into ticket CLI as a standard verb"
- When this story is complete, `ticket ready --epic=<id>` and `ticket ready --story=<id>` correctly scope output to descendants of the named parent.
  ← Satisfies: "Absorb ready computation into ticket CLI as a standard verb"
- When this story is complete, `ticket ready --format=llm` produces JSONL output with shortened keys, consistent with `ticket list --format=llm`.
  ← Satisfies: "Absorb ready computation into ticket CLI as a standard verb"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Performance] At 2,000+ ticket scale, iterating the reducer per-ticket is O(N); the sourceable lib from Epic 78fc-3858 is the scaling path — this story depends on it.
- [Maintainability] Follow the verb-sugar pattern from Epic 6f7a-4ba6: a 10-line bash shim calling a library function; no duplicated logic.
- [Testing] Test fixture with a linear dependency chain (A blocks B blocks C) confirms: initially only A is ready; after closing A, B is ready; after closing B, C is ready.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
